Narrative: Suspect 1: Light Skinned Male, Black Hair, approximately mid 20's, approximately 160 lbs, approximately 5'10, wearing a Black short sleeve shirt, black shorts and black sneakers. Suspect 2: White Male, approximately mid 20's, approximately 170 lbs, approximately 5'10, wearing a Black short sleeve shirt, tan pants, white sneakers and a black fitted hat. Suspect 3: Light skinned Male with a afro and mustache, approximately mid 20's, approximately 190 lbs, approximately 6'1, wearing a white cut off shirt, black and white sweat pants, and white crocs. On Friday, July 25, 2025, I responded to the listed location regarding a burglary that occurred the previous night, on July 24, 2025. Upon arrival, I was met by the property manager, Ms. Hernandez, and the gas attendant, Mr. Horton. They reported that last night, at approximately 22:46 hours, the bathroom was burglarized and vandalized by unknown suspects. The gas attendant locked all the doors of the business and left at 21:30 hours. The gas attendant provided me with a video of the incident. At 22:46, a Black Dodge Ram pulled into the parking lot with its back tailgate down and Suspect 1 riding on the tailgate of the truck. Once the vehicle parks, suspect 2 gets out of the driver's seat, and both suspects 1 and 2 remove a toilet bowl from the back of the truck. Suspect 1 then climbs through a broken window to the bathroom and opens the locked door from the inside of the bathroom. Suspect 3 gets out of the passenger seat and then holds the door open while Suspects 1 and 2 place the toilet bowl inside the bathroom. One of the unknown suspects then throws beans from a can on the floor of the bathroom. The suspect also smeared beans on the walls and broke the toilet paper holder. The suspects then left the bathroom leaving back the toilet bowl and fled the scene in the unknown vehicle in an unknown direction. The property manager, Ms. Hernandez, reported that nothing was taken from the property, and no other parts of the building were broken into. She further reported that the estimated amount to repair the bathroom is $1,000.00 dollars. I provided, Ms, Hernandez, with my e-mail which she can then forward me the videos of the incident which will then be forwarded to the detectives. Sgt. Donnerstag responded, took photographs of the bathroom, and also notified the detective bureau

On Saturday, July 26th, 2025, I responded to the listed location on a burglary to auto report. Upon arrival, I was met with the registered, Mr.Ortizchafla of vehicle 1 bearing New Jersey Plate N50VML, which was parked in front of 53 Euclid Avenue. The owner stated he parked his vehicle in front of 53 Euclid Avenue at 2300 hours on 07/25/2025 and locked his vehicle prior to leaving, but he left his passenger side window down a little bit enough to put an arm through it to open the door. He further stated he returned to his vehicle on 07/26/2025 at 05:30 hours and noticed his NJ Driver License, 2 Bank cards, his country license and $100.00 dollars were missing from his vehicle. He further noticed nothing else was taken and his vehicle was intact. The registered owner was advised on how to obtain a new drivers license. The vehicle couldn't be processed due to the scene being contaminated prior to my arrival, Lt. Knapp was advised. I completed a victim's notification form and provided a copy to the complainant. The complainant wanted to sign a complaint if the suspect was to be identified.

All_Incidents: Recovered Stolen Vehicle, Motor Vehicle Theft
Location: Old Hoboken Road & Blauvelt Place, Hackensack, NJ, 07601
Narrative: Suspect(s): Unknown The victim, Harishkumar A. Patel, responded to Police Headquarters on a report of a Motor Vehicle Theft. Upon arrival, the victim stated he parked his blue 4-door 2017 BMW 440I bearing NC Reg: VFJ5061 on Old Hoboken Road facing North towards Blauvelt Place on 07/27/2025 at approximately 1530 hours. The victim further stated that after parking his vehicle, he responded to Columbus Park with several friends and did not return to his vehicle until approximately 1630 hours. At the time of responding back to his vehicle, the victim did not observe his vehicle. The victim advised me that he began to check the surrounding area for his vehicle, as well as ask people in the area if they had observed his vehicle being moved or taken. The victim contacted police headquarters for assistance and P.O. Gallorini and P.O. Gibson responded to assist. Due to the victim not having the vehicle registration or any necessary vehicle information he was advised to gather the information and respond to police headquarters. The victim stated that at the time of parking his vehicle, he closed the door behind him and locked the vehicle before responding to Columbus Park. The victim further stated the master key of the vehicle was left in the center console with the sliding door shut and the vehicle kill switch activated. The victim advised me that his payments on the vehicle were all up to date, and no other party would have had access to his vehicle. There were no items of value left in the vehicle prior to the motor vehicle theft. The victim refused to have the vehicle processed. A victim notification form was completed, and a copy was given to the victim. The vehicle registration was given to dispatch and was entered into NCIC. Vehicle Insurance Information: Company: National General Policy Number: 2025652165 NAIC: 22780 A stolen vehicle entry was made into the Auto Theft Task Force website. The vehicle's estimated value is approximately $19,000 USD. While completing the necessary paperwork for the victim, I was advised by C.O. Gervasi that the vehicle was located by New Jersey State Trooper Rosen. I contacted NJ Trooper Rosen and was advised the vehicle was involved in a multi-vehicle crash on Route 80 West (6502) at approximately 1730 hours. The vehicle was then towed by New Jersey State Troopes. The vehicle was removed from NCIC.

Suspect description: White and/ or Hispanic male approximately 30 years old wearing a black baseball cap, white T-shirt, tan / beige cargo shorts, and slides shoes. Suspect vehicle description: An older gray and/ or silver Honda Accord possibly bearing New Jersey Registration K79UGW. On 7/28/25 I responded to Headquarters on a reported burglary to auto. Upon arrival, I spoke with the victim, William Barrios, who is an owner of Rent 4 Parties on 75 Atlantic Street. William reported on 7/27/25 at approximately 08:00 hours, he arrived to work and noticed all the work vehicle's doors in the listed locations parking lot were open. William stated the work vehicles appeared to be rummaged through. William also stated that all work vehicles were left unlocked overnight. The victim then checked the video surveillance cameras and observed the suspect vehicle pulled into the listed location on 7/27/25 at approximately 03:52 hours. The suspect, unknown name, was seen entering multiple vehicles taking and putting a table that is worth approximately $80.00 and an amazon drill worth approximately $50.00 into the suspect vehicle. The suspect then entered the suspect vehicle and drove off with the items at approximately 04:06 hours. The total value of stolen property is approximately $130.00. A New Jersey Victim Notification Form was completed and a copy was provided to the victim. The victim would want to sign complaints if the suspect is to be identified. The victim was advised to E-mail all video surveillance footage of the incident to mgallorini@hackensackpd.org.

Insurance: Plymouth Rock #PAC00001191262, Code 017. I responded to the listed location on a reported theft. Upon arrival, I met with the complainant (Mr. Arley) who reported parking his work van (NJ-XMGF31) in the parking lot of 286 Lodi Street facing West in the parking lot. Mr. Arley reported parking his vehicle at approximately 0800 hours on 07/28/2025. Upon his return a few hours later at 1300 hours, he observed his passenger side door slightly ajar and discovered tools missing from the rear of the van. Mr. Arley further reported the van was left locked but no forced entry or damage was observed. Stolen out of the vehicle were the following: Milwaukee Crimper $4,000 and Milwaukee cutter $3,000, Drill with batteries $2,500.00. Total stolen: $9500.00. Vehicle could not be processed at this time. No surveillance cameras were observed in the immediate area. Sgt. Competiello and Det. Tanucilli was advised of the same.
